evals.prompt: How do I access the currently authenticated user's ID and email in my controller?
evals.expected_output: Shows injecting Authentication parameter, casting principal to AuthenticatedUser record, accessing userId() and email()

evals.prompt: I need to make my /api/webhooks endpoint publicly accessible without JWT authentication. How?
evals.expected_output: Adds the path to acme.security.jwt.public-paths array in application.yml

evals.prompt: My service fails to start with 'JWT secret must be at least 64 characters'. What's the minimum required?
evals.expected_output: Explains SecurityProperties record validates secret ≥64 chars in compact constructor, shows how to set JWT_SECRET env var

evals.prompt: I want to override the default SecurityFilterChain to add custom authorization rules. Can I do this without breaking the JWT filter?
evals.expected_output: Explains @ConditionalOnMissingBean allows full override, shows how to define a custom SecurityFilterChain that still includes the JwtAuthenticationFilter

evals.prompt: What's the difference between the JWT filter in the API gateway and the one in my service? They seem to do the same thing.
evals.expected_output: Explains two-layer architecture: gateway is reactive (GlobalFilter/Mono/WebFlux), service is servlet (OncePerRequestFilter); gateway propagates X-User-Id headers, service sets SecurityContext; both validate independently
